title: That thread was brilliant Also Shinji thanking Gendo
display_title: That thread was brilliant Also Shinji thanking Gendo
wikitext:
[]: That thread was brilliant (Also: Shinji thanking Gendo???)             Quote Post by Korgan » Tue Aug 09, 2011 3:00 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3818 Great frame-by-frame analysis of 25-26! I love it, hope it gets added to the Commentary Project one day. HINT: Put some of the appropriate stuff from the soundtrack, like "Separation Anxiety", "Opening of Dream" and "Good, or Don't Be", in your playlist, read the frames and the commentary, and enjoy! I swear, it's even better than the first time. Here is a nice channel.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Tue Aug 09, 2011 3:11 pm Couldn't you just have bumped up the topic and complimented it there?

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Tue Aug 09, 2011 3:19 pm Nope! It's in the ANN Archive.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 09, 2011 3:25 pm So -- is this thread going to be be about great threads from olden days, about analysis of episodes 25 and 26 or about when if ever the commentary is going to get there?

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Tue Aug 09, 2011 3:33 pm I'd prefer the second. Specifically, what I'd like to mention is the rush of feelings which I was amazed to discover comes with those captioned images even in still form. Especially with the soundtrack on. Funny how a simple, obvious-in-hindsight idea, like "You must first accept and love yourself as you are, even if you want to change for the better and reconcile your desires and behavior" can produce such an emotional impact when it's presented artistically. ALSO: this always bugged me to hell! One of Shinji's last phrases is, "To my father, thank you!" To put it mildly, WHAT THE FLYING F*CK?! He's thanking him for... what, exactly?! Something along the lines of "I forgive you" would've fit the circumstances better...

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Tue Aug 09, 2011 3:34 pm Mr. Tines wrote: or about when if ever the commentary is going to get there?

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Aug 09, 2011 4:39 pm Even though Gendou was a complete dickie, he still thought what he did was for the best. He also helped Shinji a lot, but in subtle ways.

[]:             Quote Post by Kenshin Sephiroth » Wed Aug 10, 2011 1:06 pm I am very new to this forum, currently perusing the EvaGeeks Wiki for as much info on Eva as I can feast my eyes on, and I was just about to make a "Great Threads" request myself; until I saw THIS great thread. If anyone could follow OP suit and post links to more first-class Eva commentary/analysis, I know I would appreciate the gesture.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Wed Aug 10, 2011 1:37 pm First thing you should do is use the search button at the top and just type in topics you're unsure of. If nothing comes up that answers your questions then make a new thread about it, at which point discussion will ensue or people will link you to a previous discussion you may have missed. I'm still in favour of a thread discussing good threads, though, since I still need to find the 3 or 4 threads I found that answered questions I've forgotten the answers to.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Aug 10, 2011 1:46 pm The Threads link at the top (links to http://forum.evageeks.org/thread_lists/) gives a quick way to get an overview. The search function is frankly not very good. Making a search on Google with site:evageeks.org is more effective. The ANF archive has cherry-picked a lot of seminal threads from the period around 2004 when the advent of the DVDs, and especially the Director's Cut issues, led to a lot of paradigm shifting analyses. You can get a flavour of Shin-seiki's heavy visual work, which was responsible for many of the breakthroughs in http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3800 There we also have the grand-dady of the shipping threads http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3758 ; and some very contentious analysis http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3759

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Thu Aug 11, 2011 3:10 am Thanks a lot, Tines.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Aug 11, 2011 3:15 am Mr. Tines wrote: The search function is frankly not very good. Making a search on Google with site:evageeks.org is more effective.
